title: amaranthus retroflexus subsp. delilei
intro: Amaranthus retroflexus subsp. delilei, also known as redroot pigweed, is a subspecies of the Amaranthus retroflexus plant, a type of flowering plant in the amaranth family that is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Amaranthus retroflexus subsp. delilei include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
